description_long: This sculpture depicts a draped human figure, likely female, with intricately carved folds of fabric cascading down the body. The head and arms are missing, but the remaining torso and lower body are rendered with realistic attention to the flow and weight of the garment. The base is roughly circular and appears to be part of the same material as the figure, providing stability. The surface is weathered, with subtle discoloration and signs of age, suggesting it may have been exposed to the elements. The overall form is classical, reminiscent of Greco-Roman statuary, with a focus on the naturalistic treatment of drapery. The sculpture is monochromatic, with a pale, stone-like appearance.
description: A weathered, draped human figure sculpture with missing head and arms, rendered in pale stone with detailed fabric folds.
description_view_0: Front view showing the draped torso and detailed folds of the garment, with the head and arms missing.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the draped fabric and the elongated form of the lower body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the garment and the rounded base, with more subtle fabric details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the extended drapery and the missing upper limbs, emphasizing the sculpture's length.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting or environment is visible; the background is neutral and isolated.
